question_text: A car of mass 1000 kg has four forces acting on it: engine force 3000 N (forward), air resistance 500 N (backward), friction from wheels 800 N (backward), and a push from a tailwind of 200 N (forward). Which statement about the resultant force is correct?
options: The resultant force is 1900 N forward and the car accelerates
The resultant force is 4300 N because all forces must be added together
The resultant force is zero so the car is stationary
The resultant force is 2900 N backward
correct_answer: The resultant force is 1900 N forward and the car accelerates
explanation: Add forward forces: 3000 N + 200 N = 3200 N. Add backward forces: 500 N + 800 N = 1300 N. Resultant = 3200 N - 1300 N = 1900 N forward. Since the resultant is not zero, the forces are unbalanced and the car will accelerate forward.
